open: Publishing has a name for what happens when an author sits in the right guest chair: the podcast bump.
moment: Authors who watch their Amazon sales rank report visible spikes within 24 to 48 hours of a guest episode going live. One tracked interview converted into $10,000 in book sales.
receipt: Guest appearances on the right shows outperform standalone campaigns by up to 2.8x in brand recall, and the episode keeps selling for years because it never leaves the feed.
lesson: When the guest mentions it, the audience buys it. That is the believability transfer host-read ads borrow, at full strength.
sourceTitle: Publishing Strategy Sessions: One podcast interview, $10,000 in book sales

open: Katt Williams pulled up to Club Shay Shay, and for nearly three hours, said the quiet parts loud.
moment: He named names across comedy, and the internet did not recover for weeks.
receipt: The episode became the most-watched interview in the channel's history at 69M+ views and, per Sports Illustrated's reporting, earned Shannon Sharpe an estimated $6M and counting.
lesson: One guest, one chair, one honest conversation outperformed every entertainment ad budget that month.
sourceTitle: Sports Illustrated: Sharpe gains 'legitimacy' from record-breaking Katt Williams interview
